sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific Launches New DNA and RNA Analysis System
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific has released the Invitrogen E-Gel Power Snap Lite Electrophoresis System, a compact and integrated solution for routine nucleic acid verification. This system streamlines DNA and RNA analysis by combining gel electrophoresis and imaging in one device, eliminating manual gel casting and buffer preparation. It's designed to reduce hands-on time and improve consistency for routine and low-throughput workflows, such as PCR verification and RNA quality assessment.

feed.title: UBS (AMUB) offers autocallable buffer notes linked to Freeport-McMoRan (FCX)
feed.summary: UBS AG is offering Phoenix Autocallable Buffer Notes with Memory Interest linked to Freeport-McMoRan Inc. (FCX). These notes have a principal amount of $1,000, offer contingent interest payments of $15.80, and mature in approximately 12 months with a maturity date of June 3, 2027. The notes are automatically callable if FCX's closing price meets or exceeds the initial price, but if not called and the final price is below the downside threshold, investors may receive less than the principal amount.

feed.title: Exclusive: GM-LG battery company delays return of laid-off workers to Ohio plant
feed.summary: The Ultium Cells plant, jointly owned by General Motors and LG Energy Solution, has delayed the return of laid-off workers in Ohio until August due to weak demand for electric vehicles. Hundreds of employees were initially told to expect to return in June but received an internal announcement pushing the date back. The plant had previously laid off 850 workers temporarily and 480 permanently last fall, providing battery cells for GM's EVs.

feed.title: Bristol Myers Squibb Announces CELMoD Mezigdomide Reduces Risk of Disease Progression or Death by More than 50% vs. Standard of Care in Relapsed or Refractory Multiple Myeloma
feed.summary: Bristol Myers Squibb announced positive Phase 3 results for its CELMoD mezigdomide, demonstrating a 52% reduction in the risk of disease progression or death in patients with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma. The MeziKd combination showed a median progression-free survival of 18 months and a consistent safety profile. These findings bolster the potential of CELMoD therapies as a new standard of care and will be shared with health authorities.

feed.title: General Electric Co stock (US3696041033): GE Aerospace edges higher after new U.S. Air Force engine
feed.summary: General Electric Co (GE) shares traded modestly higher on the NYSE after GE Aerospace announced a new U.S. Air Force GE426 engine design contract, keeping the stock in focus for future defense and aviation programs. The contract involves the preliminary design review of the GE426 engine for future military applications, extending GE Aerospace's relationship with the U.S. Department of Defense. Trading near its 52-week high, the stock's performance reflects optimism about the company's aerospace-focused strategy and its role as a key defense engine supplier.

feed.title: CVS Caremark Expands GLP-1 Formulary Coverage
feed.summary: CVS Caremark announced it will reinstate Zepbound (tirzepatide) to its commercial formularies as a preferred option and remove the new-to-market block on Foundayo (orforglipron), effective October 1, 2026, and June 1, 2026, respectively. This move, stemming from negotiations with drug manufacturers, aims to expand GLP-1 access while managing costs and signals how PBMs might approach oral GLP-1 agents. The decision highlights ongoing affordability and access challenges for plan sponsors regarding weight-loss GLP-1s, with coverage often contingent on negotiated pricing concessions.

feed.title: See what symbol is on Alabama’s Coca-Cola America250 mini can
feed.summary: Coca-Cola is releasing limited-edition America250 mini cans and bottles in celebration of America's upcoming 250th birthday in 2026. Each state will have a unique design, with Alabama's can featuring an "AL" abbreviation and a football helmet, honoring its strong football tradition. These collectible cans, available at major retailers, also offer interactive experiences and prizes.

feed.title: [U.S. Equities] Dow Inc rises for second consecutive session, up 165 points; Nasdaq also hits new record high (as of morning, 29th)
feed.summary: Dow Inc. (DOW) saw its stock rise for the second consecutive session, gaining 165 points on the morning of the 29th. The Nasdaq also achieved a new record high. This positive movement indicates a strong performance for both Dow Inc. and the broader Nasdaq market.

feed.title: Oracle Corp Stock (ORCL) Moved Up by 6.39% on May 29: Facts Behind the Movement
feed.summary: Oracle's stock price increased by 6.39% due to a new multi-billion dollar cloud infrastructure deal with the US government for AI computing capacity. The company also raised its revenue guidance for fiscal year 2027, reflecting strong demand for its AI infrastructure and cloud offerings, and has received positive sentiment from financial analysts and increased institutional investor confidence. Despite these positives, Oracle faces risks including substantial net debt, lower profitability in its AI cloud operations, uncertainty in converting its RPO backlog, and significant restructuring costs from recent workforce reductions.

feed.title: Duke Energy Florida implements third rate reduction to lower residential customer bills by approximately 25% in 2026
feed.summary: Duke Energy Florida is implementing its third rate reduction of 2026, which will decrease residential customer bills by approximately $50, or 25%, for every 1,000 kilowatt-hours used, when compared to January levels. This reduction is attributed to the early removal of storm cost recovery charges, a seasonal decrease to help with higher energy use periods, and a "true up" reflecting the difference between collected and incurred storm costs. Additionally, the company highlights ongoing efforts to create further savings through efficiency improvements in natural gas plants, new solar energy sites, and the passing on of Inflation Reduction Act tax credits.

feed.title: Cisco's Biggest AI Bet Wasn't Made In 2025 — It Was Made In 2016
feed.summary: Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins revealed that the company's significant role in the current AI buildout stems from a silicon company acquisition made in 2016, long before AI gained mainstream attention. This strategic move provided Cisco with critical networking silicon, a key competitive advantage for connecting large AI clusters. Ironically, while Cisco aimed to shift towards software and recurring revenue, the AI boom has emphasized hardware, underscoring that many AI winners had laid foundational groundwork years in advance.

feed.title: Estonia clears Tesla FSD for roads, company says rollout to begin soon
feed.summary: Estonia's transport authority has approved Tesla's FSD (Full Self-Driving) driver-assistance system for use on its roads, recognizing a type approval initially granted in the Netherlands. Tesla announced on X that the rollout will begin soon. The authority clarified that FSD is a Level 2 driver assistance system, meaning the driver remains fully responsible and must be prepared to take control at all times.

feed.title: SLB and Vår Energi embark on digital quest to speed up oil & gas decision-making
feed.summary: SLB and Vår Energi are expanding their collaboration to accelerate oil and gas decision-making, aiming to cut the time from discovery to first oil by leveraging collaborative well planning and integrated field development planning. They plan to use the Delfi digital platform in a cloud-native environment to connect various stages of development, enabling concurrent work with shared data. This strategy is expected to reduce cycle times from months to days, improve consistency, and manage complex offshore assets more effectively.

feed.title: Chevron (NYSE: CVX) investors back board, reject ESG proposals
feed.summary: Chevron Corporation's stockholders voted to back the company's board of directors and executive compensation at their 2026 Annual Meeting, with high approval ratings for all 12 director nominees and the auditor ratification. Conversely, all environmental, social, and governance (ESG) related proposals, including those concerning an independent chair and human rights reports, were rejected with very low support. This outcome signifies strong investor confidence in Chevron's current leadership and operational strategy over increased ESG oversight.

feed.title: CoStar to buy Zonda for $800M
feed.summary: CoStar Group is set to acquire Zonda for $800 million, continuing its strategy of growth through acquisitions. This deal was reported by the Washington Business Journal and follows a series of other acquisitions by CoStar. The article is a brief overview and highlights the ongoing expansion of CoStar's business operations.

feed.title: International Business Machines' (IBM) Outperform Rating Reaffirmed at Wedbush
feed.summary: Wedbush has reaffirmed an "outperform" rating and a $320 price target for International Business Machines (IBM), indicating a potential 12% upside. This aligns with a broader analyst consensus of "Moderate Buy" and a $294.41 price target, supported by IBM's recent better-than-expected quarterly results. The company also announced major investments in AI-driven open-source software security and quantum computing research, contributing positively to market sentiment.

feed.title: CoStar to acquire Zonda for $800M
feed.summary: CoStar Group announced its plan to acquire homebuilding data and software firm Zonda for $800 million in cash. This acquisition aims to expand CoStar's presence in the homebuilding sector, leveraging Zonda's data, software, and online marketplaces for land acquisition, development planning, and home sales. The deal, expected to close in the second half of 2026, will also integrate Zonda Envision with CoStar's recently acquired Matterport 3D digital twin technology, creating cross-selling opportunities across various real estate segments.

feed.title: CFG: Strategic growth, tech-driven efficiency, and risk discipline drive strong financial outlook
feed.summary: The article highlights Citizens Financial Group's (CFG) strong financial outlook driven by economic resilience, strategic growth in affluent consumer and commercial banking, and efficiency initiatives leveraging AI. The company aims for ambitious financial targets through 2027 by expanding in private banking and focusing on risk management. This information is based on their Bernstein 42nd Annual Strategic Decisions Conference Audio Transcript.

feed.title: Prudential (NYSE: PUK) AGM backs all resolutions after vote correction
feed.summary: Prudential plc announced that all resolutions put forth at its Annual General Meeting on May 28, 2026, were passed with strong shareholder majorities, following a correction to the total votes cast as a percentage of issued share capital. Shareholders approved the 2025 Annual Report, the revised Directors' Remuneration Policy, elected and re-elected directors, and renewed authorities for share allotment and buybacks. The company reported robust shareholder participation, with total votes cast representing 79.30% of the issued share capital.

feed.title: AbbVie (ABBV) Expands Venclyxto Label for Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia Treatment
feed.summary: AbbVie (ABBV) recently received European Commission approval to expand the label for its drug Venclyxto, allowing its use with acalabrutinib and ibrutinib for previously untreated adult patients with chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL). While the company shows strong profitability and growth, indicated by a GF Score of 79/100, its high P/E ratio and recent insider selling activity may indicate potential overvaluation and warrant investor caution.

feed.title: Replimune to Resubmit RP1 BLA; FDA to Prioritize Review for Advanced Melanoma
feed.summary: Replimune will resubmit its Biologics License Application (BLA) for RP1 with nivolumab for advanced melanoma after productive discussions with the FDA. The FDA has agreed to prioritize the review of this resubmission, acknowledging the significant unmet need for patients with limited treatment options. This decision is supported by data from the IGNYTE trial, which evaluated the combination therapy in patients who had progressed on anti-PD-1 therapy.

feed.title: Target Hires New Chief Legal & Compliance Officer
feed.summary: Target has appointed Grant McGee as its new EVP and chief legal and compliance officer, effective May 31. McGee brings 20 years of legal expertise and will oversee legal, compliance, risk, and government affairs functions. This appointment comes as Target experiences encouraging growth, with recent first-quarter net sales up 6.7% and comparable-digital sales growing 8.9%.

feed.title: Palomar’s earthquake reinsurance limit up to $3.92bn after June renewal
feed.summary: Palomar Holdings has completed its June 1st reinsurance placement, increasing its earthquake coverage to $3.92 billion and continental US hurricane coverage to $135 million. The company secured an additional $421 million in limit, including $360 million from its seventh Torrey Pines Re catastrophe bond. Palomar also raised its full-year 2026 adjusted net income guidance to $266 million–$280 million.

feed.title: Allegion plc stock (IE00BFRT3W74): shares edge higher after latest price move on NYSE
feed.summary: Allegion plc shares saw a modest upward move on the New York Stock Exchange on May 29, 2026, trading higher in regular session dealing. This keeps the Ireland-domiciled security business within its recent trading range ahead of the next earnings catalyst. The company's positioning in locks and access-control technologies makes valuation metrics like P/E and EV/EBITDA crucial for assessing the stock.

feed.title: RTX Corporation (NYSE: RTX) Holds Steady As Earnings Estimates, Revenue Forecasts Signal Modest Growth
feed.summary: RTX Corporation (NYSE: RTX) is maintaining a steady performance, with analysts projecting modest growth in earnings and revenue. The company holds a Zacks Rank of No. 3 (Hold), indicating it is expected to perform in line with the broader market. Recent financial reports show RTX has consistently surpassed consensus EPS and revenue estimates.

feed.title: RTX: Record backlog and global reach drive growth, supported by innovation and risk management
feed.summary: RTX is positioned for growth due to a record $271 billion backlog and an expanding global presence, catering to strong demand in aerospace and defense. The company's operational initiatives and risk management strategies are key to sustaining its performance and fostering innovation. This summary is AI-generated and refers to Slides Release from May 29, 2026.

feed.title: Ryman Hospitality Properties stock hits 52-week high at $116.90
feed.summary: Ryman Hospitality Properties (RHP) stock recently hit a 52-week high of $116.67, with shares currently trading at $117.17, reflecting a 25.11% increase over the past year. The company reported strong Q1 2026 financial results, surpassing analyst expectations for EPS and revenue, and declared a second-quarter cash dividend of $1.20 per share. Despite the stock trading near its 52-week high, InvestingPro analysis suggests it may be overvalued relative to its Fair Value estimate.

feed.title: Pebblebrook Hotel Trust stock hits 52-week high at $15.73
feed.summary: Pebblebrook Hotel Trust (PEB) stock has reached a new 52-week high of $15.73, marking a 67.8% appreciation over the past year and a 37.5% year-to-date return. This surge follows strong Q1 2026 earnings, outperforming revenue and EPS forecasts, and an upgrade to "Buy" from Truist Securities with an increased price target of $17.00. InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock remains undervalued despite its recent gains.

feed.title: [144] TAKE TWO INTERACTIVE SOFTWARE INC SEC Filing
feed.summary: This article details a Form 144 SEC filing by Take-Two Interactive Software Inc. (TTWO) regarding the proposed sale of securities. The filing indicates a sale of 131 common shares with an aggregate market value of $28,592.06 through Fidelity Brokerage Services LLC on NASDAQ, stemming from restricted stock vesting. The document also includes standard SEC Form 144 disclosures and past SEC filings and news for the company.

feed.title: REAX: Board and auditor appointments confirmed, all motions approved, and focus set on growth
feed.summary: The article reports on The Real Brokerage, Inc. (REAX) 2026 Annual General Meeting (AGM). During the meeting, board and auditor appointments were confirmed, financials were reviewed, and a focus on shareholder value and industry leadership was outlined. All motions were passed, and no shareholder questions were raised.

feed.title: Duke Energy Florida implements third rate reduction to lower residential customer bills by approximately 25% in 2026
feed.summary: Duke Energy Florida is implementing its third rate reduction of 2026, which will lower residential customer bills by approximately 25% (or $50 per 1,000 kWh) from June through September. This reduction includes savings from the early removal of storm cost recovery charges, a March decrease for higher energy use periods, and a "true up" of storm costs. The company also highlights other savings initiatives like efficiency improvements in natural gas plants and the passing on of Inflation Reduction Act tax credits.

feed.title: Intel Corp Stock (INTC) Opened Up by 4.06% on May 29: What Investors Need To Know
feed.summary: Intel Corp (INTC) stock rose by 4.06% on May 29, outperforming its sector. This increase is attributed to recent product innovations like the Arc G-Series processors, potential foundry deals with major tech companies, and strong Q1 2026 financial results driven by demand in artificial intelligence. Despite some analyst concerns about valuation and competition, the company's turnaround strategy and positive outlook are boosting investor confidence.

feed.title: International Business Machines Corp Stock (IBM) Opened Up by 4.46% on May 29: A Full Analysis
feed.summary: International Business Machines (IBM) stock surged 4.46% today due to strategic announcements including a $10 billion investment in quantum computing and a $5 billion commitment to open-source software security through Project Lightwell. Analysts responded positively, raising price targets and highlighting IBM's strong financial performance and efforts to "future-proof" the company through critical emerging technologies. The company also reaffirmed its 2026 outlook with a projected 5% revenue growth.

feed.title: Qualcomm Inc Stock (QCOM) Opened Up by 3.48% on May 29: Key Drivers Unveiled
feed.summary: Qualcomm (QCOM) stock rose 3.48% on May 29, primarily driven by strategic moves into AI, including a deal to supply custom AI chips to ByteDance and the launch of the Snapdragon C Platform for AI-enabled laptops. Despite positive developments, the stock experienced intraday volatility due to mixed guidance, concerns over Android smartphone demand, and some analyst caution regarding short-term outlook and valuation. Technical indicators show a buy signal with a MACD of 19.01, though some market sentiment remains bearish.

feed.title: Mathew Watson (BBY) sells 3,298 shares for $211,136 under Rule 144
feed.summary: Mathew Watson, an insider at Best Buy Co Inc (BBY), sold 3,298 shares of common stock for $211,135.64 on March 23, 2026, as detailed in a Form 144 SEC filing. The sale followed a stock option exercise on March 11, 2025. Fidelity Brokerage Services LLC handled the transaction, which is publicly disclosed under Rule 144 regulations.

feed.title: IBM, Salesforce Share Gains Contribute To Dow's 225-Point Climb
feed.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose by 225 points, primarily driven by gains in IBM and Salesforce shares. IBM's stock increased by 1.6% while Salesforce saw a 1.2% rise, contributing significantly to the index's upward movement.

feed.title: AMETEK Inc stock (US0311001004): steady trading ahead of next catalysts
feed.summary: AMETEK Inc. (US0311001004) stock traded steadily on the NYSE, in line with the broader U.S. industrials sector on May 29, 2026, as investors continued to process the company's May quarterly earnings and outlook. The stock's performance reflects a market weighing the company's financial figures and forward-looking commentary within the context of U.S. industrial and technology-linked cyclicals. Future investor focus will likely be on upcoming company updates, sector data, and macro indicators to confirm or challenge current valuation assumptions.

feed.title: SK telecom surges past KT on AI-driven stock rally
feed.summary: SK telecom has significantly outpaced its rival KT Corporation in market value this year, primarily driven by investor excitement over artificial intelligence. While KT's stock gained only 1.17%, SK telecom's shares surged 81.80% year-to-date, reversing an earlier market capitalization lead held by KT. Analysts attribute SK telecom's strong performance and future growth potential to its advancements in AI-related businesses, physical AI, and quantum cryptography communications, with expectations of robust earnings in the coming quarters.

feed.title: RLGT (NYSE) insider reports 33,540-share sale, RSUs vesting in cash
feed.summary: An insider at Radiant Logistics (RLGT) has filed a Form 144, reporting sales of 33,540 shares on May 27, 2026, for $285,214 and 7,500 shares on March 31, 2026, for $52,500. The filing also indicates multiple Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) vesting on May 28, 2026, which will be settled in cash. The insider identified in the filing is Todd Macomber.

feed.title: Blackstone Is About to Take a 54% Loss on Iconic Seattle Tower
feed.summary: Blackstone Inc. is selling the US Bank Center in Seattle for approximately $280 million, representing a 54% loss from its original purchase price seven years ago. This deal highlights the growing trend of commercial landlords accepting significant losses in the current real estate market. Spear Street Capital is reportedly the buyer, though the deal's closure depends on financing.

feed.title: Prediction: AI Rally Exhaustion Could Send Rambus Lower
feed.summary: Rambus (RMBS) shares have experienced a significant surge due to AI memory demand, with strong revenue growth in Q1 2026. However, the stock's current valuation at a 68 P/E multiple is stretched given decelerating growth, and insider selling by the CEO and board members signals potential concerns. Analysts predict a downside for RMBS, with a price target significantly lower than its current trading price, suggesting the AI rally might be exhausting for this particular stock.

feed.title: What To Do After Dell's Breakout Boosted Hewlett-Packard Enterprise Stock (NYSE:HPE)
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Company (HPE) is rated a Buy due to surging AI/server demand and networking momentum, benefiting from Dell's strong earnings and subsequent analyst upgrades. Citi raised its price target for HPE to $39, citing similar tailwinds in AI and server markets. The author plans to upgrade HPE to a Strong Buy if upcoming quarterly results are robust and shares avoid profit-taking sell-offs.

feed.title: Tyler Technologies Secures $1 Billion Five-Year Unsecured Revolving Credit Facility Led by Wells Fargo
feed.summary: Tyler Technologies has secured a new $1.0 billion unsecured revolving credit facility with Wells Fargo, maturing in May 2031. This new facility replaces a previous $700 million revolver and is intended for general corporate purposes including acquisitions and capital expenditures. The refinancing increases liquidity and extends the company's financial maturity.

feed.title: NetApp, Inc. Stock 12‑Month Price Target Raised to $141.57, Implies 1% Downside
feed.summary: NetApp, Inc. (NTAP) stock's average 12-month price target has increased to $141.57 from $116.86, based on estimates from 14 analysts. This updated target suggests a 1% potential downside from the May 28 closing price. The consensus rating for NetApp has shifted to "Hold" among 22 analysts.

feed.title: NYSE parent ICE pushes ‘level playing field’ for 24/7 onchain perps
feed.summary: Intercontinental Exchange (ICE), parent company of the NYSE, is advocating for regulatory changes to permit regulated exchanges to offer 24/7 onchain perpetual futures trading. ICE CEO Jeffrey Sprecher highlighted the success of decentralized exchanges like Hyperliquid and discussed potential synergies between crypto and traditional finance. This push follows ICE's investment in cryptocurrency exchange OKX and the NYSE's partnership with Securitize to explore blockchain-based stock trading infrastructure.

feed.title: V2X, Inc. Stock 12‑Month Price Target Raised to $80.67
feed.summary: V2X, Inc.'s average 12-month price target has increased from $79.42 to $80.67, based on estimates from 12 analysts. The price forecasts range from $70 to $94 per share, and the consensus rating remains a "Buy" with 7 Buys, 4 Holds, and 1 Sell.

feed.title: Ambarella, Inc. Stock 12‑Month Price Target Raised to $91, Implies 1% Downside
feed.summary: Ambarella, Inc.'s average 12-month price target has been raised to $91 from $87.92, according to estimates from 13 analysts. This updated target suggests a 1% potential downside based on the May 28 closing price. The consensus rating for the stock remains "Buy" among 16 analysts.

feed.title: MongoDB, Inc. Stock 12‑Month Price Target Raised to $382, Implies 17% Upside
feed.summary: Analysts have raised the average 12-month price target for MongoDB, Inc. (MDB) stock to $382, up from $362.81, implying a 17% potential upside based on the May 28 closing price. The consensus rating from 40 analysts remains "Buy," with 31 recommending Buy and 9 recommending Hold.
